name: Irenidae
description: Fairy-bluebirds are a small family of strikingly colored passerine birds found in tropical forests of South and Southeast Asia. They are known for their brilliant blue and black plumage and frugivorous diet.

children.name: Irena
children.description: A genus of brilliantly blue and black passerines that feed primarily on fruit in tropical forests.

children.name: Irena puella
children.description: The Asian Fairy-bluebird has brilliant ultramarine upperparts and black underparts in males, while females are dull blue. It inhabits evergreen forests from India to the Philippines and Indonesia.

children.name: Irena cyanogastra
children.description: The Philippine Fairy-bluebird has bright blue upperparts and blue underparts, with a black face and throat. It is endemic to the Philippines, inhabiting lowland and montane forests.

children.name: Irena tweeddalii
children.description: The Palawan Fairy-bluebird has glossy blue upperparts, blue underparts, and a black face. It is endemic to Palawan in the Philippines, inhabiting lowland rainforests.
